layman_headline: Hyperliquid pulls back sharply in a fearful market — creating a potential bounce opportunity.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Fear' mode with Bitcoin dropping below $63K after geopolitical tensions involving Iran. Hyperliquid (HYPE), a major decentralized exchange token, has fallen about 12% in a week despite having one of the strongest chart structures among all candidates. This kind of pullback in an otherwise bullish trend can create a buying opportunity for swing traders who catch the bounce before the crowd returns.
layman_action: Consider a small position in HYPE around $59-60 with a strict stop-loss at $55.50. If it doesn't bounce within a few days, cut losses quickly. This is a higher-risk play in a nervous market.
expert_reasoning: HYPE stands out with a chart bias score of +1.10 (bullish), the highest among all candidates. The 5-day momentum is still positive (+2.2%) despite the -11.7% weekly drawdown, and price remains above its 20-day SMA — a moderately bullish structure. The bullish Marubozu candlestick pattern signals pure buying conviction. RSI at 59 is elevated but not dangerously overbought; per our signal intelligence, RSI has an 80% win rate and this reading combined with a pullback into support is a classic swing setup. The Fear & Greed index at 27 (improving from 25) creates a contrarian backdrop. The main risk is BTC weakness from geopolitical headlines (Iran strikes, China uncertainty), but HYPE's high volume ($639M) ensures liquidity for a clean exit.
risk_factors: BTC dropping further below $63K on Iran/China geopolitical escalation would drag HYPE lower regardless of chart strength
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising indicates capital rotating away from altcoins — headwind for any alt trade
RSI at 59 after a big run means HYPE could still have correction room to mid-$50s before finding real support
watch_list: BTC price action around $62K-63K support — a breakdown here invalidates most alt trades
HYPE holding $60 support zone over the next 24-48 hours
Fear & Greed index — if it drops back to 20-22, defer entry and wait for stabilization
market_context: The crypto market is in Fear territory (27/100) with BTC under $63K following a U.S. strike on Iran and Trump's China comments adding uncertainty. Bitcoin ETFs have seen $368M in inflows over 3 days, which provides some floor, but altcoins are bleeding as BTC dominance holds above 56%.
context_banner: Geopolitical shock drives a broad crypto pullback, but HYPE's bullish chart structure and healthy volume make it the strongest bounce candidate in a fearful market.

key_signals.description: HYPE RSI at 59 after a -12% pullback into an uptrend — our highest-confidence signal type (80% win rate) supports a bounce entry.
HYPE has the highest chart bias score (+1.10) of all candidates with bullish Marubozu candle and price above key moving averages.
Fear & Greed at 27 and improving from 25 — contrarian indicator suggests fear is near a local peak, supporting dip-buying.
Bitcoin under $63K after Iran strike creates broad selloff, but HYPE-specific news (derivatives reset) suggests oversold conditions in exchange tokens.
Bitcoin ETFs adding $368M in 3-day buying streak provides a macro floor, reducing risk of a complete market collapse during this pullback.
